summary: Whitney Wolfe Herd discusses her journey from a traumatic exit at Tinder to founding Bumble. She emphasizes the importance of authenticity, turning personal pain into collective purpose, and why true success requires pure intention rather than revenge.

principles.statement: Self-worth does not equal net worth.
principles.rationale: Society's standards of wealth do not equate to spiritual or personal fulfillment; you can be financially wealthy but spiritually bankrupt.
principles.source_quote: I will tell you that on paper at my on paper wealthiest according to society standards I was spiritually the poorest I've ever been

principles.statement: Turn personal pain into collective strength.
principles.rationale: Moving your mindset from 'me' to 'we' gives you the perseverance to build something meaningful and endure the inevitable hardships of entrepreneurship.
principles.source_quote: I'm going to take what almost killed me and I'm going to make us stronger

principles.statement: Life and business require both intention and attention.
principles.rationale: A pure intention is the necessary foundation, but it cannot thrive without extreme focus, persistence, and daily nurturing.
principles.source_quote: Life is about two things intention and attention. The intention we set... is the foundation of everything but it can't stop there you know you have to give it extreme attention and focus and persistence

principles.statement: The right intention gives you energy rather than taking it.
principles.rationale: When you are serving the greater good of humanity, the purpose fuels you to keep going even when you are exhausted by rejections and setbacks.
principles.source_quote: I fundamentally believe that the right intention will give you energy it won't take it from you

frameworks.name: Processing Hurt Feelings
frameworks.when_to_apply: When feeling rejected, judged, or hurt by others' opinions.
steps: Acknowledge the hurt feelings without shaming yourself for having them.
Observe the feeling objectively as a wounded part of yourself.
Accept these pieces so the emotion doesn't take over your life.
frameworks.source_quote: I don't shame myself for having hurt feelings... I just kind of look at it and I stare at it and say okay that's okay that's like a wounded part of you... and we'll accept all these pieces and so that way doesn't take over your life

frameworks.name: The 'Why' Test for Business
frameworks.when_to_apply: When starting a business or when a business is facing severe setbacks and you are considering giving up.
steps: Ask yourself: 'Who is this serving?' and 'Who is this helping?'
Determine if the business solves a problem for the greater good of humanity.
If the answer is only to make money for yourself, stop and reset your intention.
frameworks.source_quote: I always ask people what's the why like why are you doing it like what's what is the bigger what can this do for people like what is this solving

mental_models.name: The Spiritual Plug
mental_models.description: Viewing spirituality and personal alignment as an electrical cord that must remain plugged into a power source to keep you balanced.
mental_models.example: When she feels off-kilter, she recognizes the 'plug comes out of the wall' and actively reconnects through meditation, nature, and genuine connection.

mental_models.name: The Mask of Inauthenticity
mental_models.description: The concept that humans are born deeply authentic but gradually put on a mask to shield themselves from judgment, rejection, or disapproval, ultimately losing their true selves.

heuristics: What almost killed me can make us stronger.
You just have to go in forward motion.
If you're doing it just to make money, stop and reset.

signature_quotes.text: I've come to realize that selfworth does not equal net worth.
signature_quotes.context: Discussing how she felt spiritually bankrupt despite being a billionaire on paper.

signature_quotes.text: Revenge is not a plot for Success.
signature_quotes.context: Refuting the narrative that she built Bumble to get back at her former Tinder co-founders.

signature_quotes.text: I'm going to take what almost killed me and I'm going to make us stronger.
signature_quotes.context: Describing her realization on the bathroom floor after leaving Tinder.

signature_quotes.text: Courage actually can't exist with the absence of fear you have to be scared to be courageous.
signature_quotes.context: Reflecting on having to build a new company while simultaneously dealing with a public lawsuit and trauma.

signature_quotes.text: There is no such thing worth fighting for that only serves ourselves.
signature_quotes.context: Explaining why businesses must have a purpose beyond personal financial gain.

anti_patterns.description: Building a business out of revenge or a vendetta.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It drains your energy and is a toxic foundation; true success requires pure intention and serving the greater good.

anti_patterns.description: People-pleasing and obsessing over being liked by everyone.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It acts as an unpaid full-time job that physically, spiritually, and mentally bankrupts you.

anti_patterns.description: Starting a business solely to make money for yourself.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: When things get hard, you won't have the deeper purpose required to persevere through the setbacks and exhaustion.
